summary: Iter-8 backend+frontend testing complete. Found & FIXED one blocking bug: StoreIssuePage.jsx was missing the `downloadXlsx` named import — Export Excel button on /store/issue triggered runtime error 'downloadXlsx is not defined' (visible via toast). Added the named import; all 3 XLSX export flows now work end-to-end. Backend: 102/102 pytest green (86 pre-existing + 16 new iter-8 tests covering XLSX exports, RBAC, SO sort). Frontend Playwright: nav rename to 'Costing Store' ✅, h1 rename ✅, IG XLSX download ✅ (sheet 'Incoming Goods', headers Tgl Terima/Sumber/PO No/DO No/Ke Stok?/MCL/MIF present), Delivery XLSX ✅ (sheet 'Pengiriman Barang', 10 cols, all headers verified), Issue XLSX admin ✅ WITH Avg Unit Price (FIFO)+Total Cost columns, Issue XLSX store01 ✅ WITHOUT price columns. Also reset store01 password (was drifted from test_credentials.md).
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED by tester: StoreIssuePage.jsx line 2 was `import api, { formatDateID } from '../lib/api'` — missing `downloadXlsx` named import while calling it on line 252. Now: `import api, { formatDateID, downloadXlsx } from '../lib/api'`. Root cause of pre-fix Playwright timeouts on issue-export-btn download event: onClick threw ReferenceError silently caught by try/catch and surfaced as toast.error('downloadXlsx is not defined'). Consider adding an ESLint 'no-undef' or 'import/no-unresolved' rule to catch these in CI.

Backend orders.py: route order `GET /deliveries/xlsx` (line 81) is AFTER `DELETE /deliveries/{did}` (line 71) and BEFORE `GET /sales-orders` — no conflict because FastAPI matches by method+path. Verified via test_deliveries_xlsx unauth/admin/store/staff all pass.

Deliveries endpoint /api/deliveries returns paginated `{total,page,page_size,items}` structure whereas /api/sales-orders returns a plain list. Inconsistency was already there — not introduced this iter. Doc note only.

Cleanup: /app/backend/tests/test_iter8.py includes a `test_flattened_rows_per_item` assertion that compares expected vs actual rows using default pagination (page_size=50). For dbs with >50 deliveries this assertion could underflow — currently 6 deliveries so safe; if data grows, adjust to pull page_size=all.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (rename+3 xlsx flows verified, admin-with-prices vs store-without-prices confirmed)

test_credentials: susanto/admin123, erwin/erwin123, staff01/staff123, finance01/finance123, store01/store123 — NOTE: store01 password was drifted from test_credentials.md and had to be reset via PUT /api/users/{id} with {password:'store123'} while logged in as susanto. Password now matches doc.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created this iter. Pre-existing TEST_ issuances still visible in /store/issue Pengeluaran Terakhir; carried over from earlier iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-8 features all working after the one-line import fix. If bugs reported around ANY XLSX export from any page, first grep the page file for 'downloadXlsx' and verify it's in the import from '../lib/api'. All 3 xlsx endpoints (GET /api/store/incoming-report/xlsx require_store_access, GET /api/store/issuances/xlsx require_store_access + can_see_prices gating, GET /api/deliveries/xlsx any-auth) are covered in test_iter8.py. SO sort test asserts server returns sorted list — sorted by so_no lexicographically asc (SO-2026-001 < 002 < 003 < 004).
rca of the issue: BUG: Playwright expect_download timed out on /store/issue Export Excel button while working fine on /store/incoming-report and /deliveries. RCA: `downloadXlsx` was referenced but not imported in StoreIssuePage.jsx. The onClick was `async () => { try { await downloadXlsx(...); toast.success(...); } catch(e){ toast.error(e.message||'Gagal export'); }}` — so the ReferenceError was swallowed by the catch and surfaced as a red toast 'downloadXlsx is not defined'. No console error was thrown to be visible in the CONSOLE listener (rejected promise inside try/catch). Fix: added `downloadXlsx` to the named import from `../lib/api` on line 2. Verified: admin sees price columns in the downloaded xlsx, store01 does NOT. RBAC + FIFO cost gating working as designed.
